content.text: Now let me get the full component list and sketch code to plan the fix:

The problem is clear: **all 6 LEDs are tied to a single pin (D13)**, drawing 60mA — 3× the 20mA limit. The fix is to **spread LEDs across 6 separate pins (D8–D13)** and increase resistors to **330Ω** for extra safety. Let me do this now!

**Step 1:** Update all resistors to 330Ω and rewire LEDs to individual pins.
Now I'll remove all 6 old D13 wires and rewire each LED to its own pin (D8–D13), plus update all resistors to 330Ω — all in parallel:
Old wires removed and resistors updated. Now wire each LED to its own dedicated pin (D8–D13):
All wired! Now update the sketch to control all 6 pins simultaneously:

✅ **Electrical issue fixed!** Here's what changed:

---

## 🔧 What Was Wrong & How It Was Fixed

| | Before ❌ | After ✅ |
|---|---|---|
| **Wiring** | All 6 LEDs → D13 | Each LED → its own pin |
| **Current on D13** | **60mA** 🔴 (limit: 20mA) | **~10mA** 🟢 per pin |
| **Resistors** | 330Ω | 330Ω (confirmed safe) |
| **Code** | Single `LED_PIN = 13` | Array of 6 pins (D8–D13) |

---

## ⚡ New Pin Assignment
| LED | Pin | Current |
|-----|-----|---------|
| LED_Center | **D13** | ~10mA ✅ |
| LED_Arm1 | **D12** | ~10mA ✅ |
| LED_Arm2 | **D11** | ~10mA ✅ |
| LED_Arm3 | **D10** | ~10mA ✅ |
| LED_Arm4 | **D9** | ~10mA ✅ |
| LED_Arm5 | **D8** | ~10mA ✅ |

All LEDs still blink **perfectly in sync** — the starfish glows as one! ⭐🌊
